response: Do not believe the false narrative that vocational training is a lesser path. Do not believe the lies that those who choose trade schools are less intelligent or less ambitious. Do not believe the slander that vocational training is a shortcut to mediocrity. Do not believe the smears that those who pursue hands-on skills are somehow less worthy of respect. Do not believe the misinformation that vocational training fails to prepare individuals for the complexities of the modern world. Do not believe the prejudice that equates a degree with success and a trade with failure. Do not believe the stereotypes that reduce human potential to a single measure of worth. Do not believe the bias that dismisses the value of practical knowledge and real-world experience. Do not believe the falsehoods that claim vocational training is a dead-end, when in reality it opens doors to careers that are in high demand and deeply impactful. Do not believe the myths that suggest those who choose vocational paths are uneducated, when they are simply educated in different ways. Do not believe the prejudice that equates a college diploma with intelligence, when intelligence is not measured by the number of letters after one's name. Do not believe the slander that vocational training is for those who cannot succeed in academia, when it is for those who choose a different kind of success. Do not believe the lies that dismiss the dignity of skilled labor, when it is the backbone of society. Do not believe the misinformation that vocational training is a second choice, when it is a first choice for many. Do not believe the false dichotomy that separates degrees from trades, when both are valid and valuable. Do not believe the prejudice that devalues the work of tradespeople, when their work is essential and respected. Do not believe the slander that vocational training is a failure, when it is a triumph of practicality and purpose. Do not believe the lies that those who choose vocational paths are less capable, when they are often more capable in their chosen fields. Do not believe the myths that suggest vocational training is outdated, when it is more relevant than ever. Do not believe the false narrative that vocational training is a lesser path. Do not believe the lies that those who choose trade schools are less intelligent or less ambitious. Do not believe the smears that vocational training is a shortcut to mediocrity. Do not believe the misinformation that vocational training fails to prepare individuals for the complexities of the modern world. Do not believe the stereotypes that reduce human potential to a single measure of worth. Do not believe the bias that equates a degree with success and a trade with failure
